rulings.Case Title: MILENA MAIONCHI VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday December 13, 2018 line 1. PETITIONER MILENA MAIONCHI Notice Of Motion For A Writ Of Administrative Mandamus GRANTED. This dispute centers on the interpretation of state law. Accordingly, the Court reviewed de novo and based on that review GRANTS the petition. The Court finds the newly constructed second floor-and the unit contained on it- constitute "new construction" for purposes of Civil Code § 1954.52(a)(1). The Planning Code in effect in 2003 permitted Petitioner to convert the studio into commercial space as a matter of right, and as a general matter, did not require the converted studio to be replaced with a new unit. This is new construction. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SCOTT FRIEDMAN ET AL VS. MICHAEL CARBOY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Otice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday December 13, 2018 line 4. PLAINTIFF SCOTT FRIEDMAN, REBECCA KADEN Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint DENIED without prejudice. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1324(b). Declaration in support of the motion consists of three paragraphs, none of which contain the requisite specifications required by CRC 3.1324(b). Additionally, Plaintiff's proposed cause of action for breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing is premature. Plaintiff's argument that new causes of action may be added into the action without compliance with ADR is not supported by any authority. Initial compliance covered the action as it was previously filed, i.e. covered one cause of action. Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that any number of newly alleged causes of action is covered by the initial compliance. The Court notes that Defendant's advanced demurrer-like arguments in the opposition. While the Court will not make a ruling on those arguments at this point, in any renewed motion for leave to amend Plaintiff should address well-taken arguments to avoid unnecessary motion practice in the future. = (501/REQ)
